title: Capurodendron bakeri
taxonomy: Capurodendron bakeri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. This family includes over 140 genera of trees and shrubs, commonly known as the soapberry or soapbark family. Capurodendron bakeri is a member of the genus Capurodendron, which consists of several species native to Madagascar. The plant is a part of the order Sapindales, a group of flowering plants characterized by their five-petaled flowers and winged fruit. Further classification of Capurodendron bakeri includes its kingdom as Plantae, a broad grouping that encompasses all vascular plants.

intro: Capurodendron bakeri, also known as the Baker's capuro tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Capurodendron bakeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
